concept: Boil / Heat Liquid to Sustained Bubbling
description: To heat a liquid, or food in liquid, until bubbles form and rise continuously through it. The verb also describes the liquid reaching that state without naming an outside agent. 'pharelo' (cook) covers heat preparation broadly; 'therapi' identifies the moving liquid state.
sound_symbolism: Soft dental breath carries 'the' into balanced 'e'. Stressed 'ra' rolls the tongue through broad 'a', then final 'pi' closes the lips and rises to bright 'i'. A swelling center breaks into a small sharp finish.
grammatical_notes: A transitive or intransitive verb: 'mia phialu therapi.' (I boil water.) The vessel and heat source fit ordinary phrases, as do duration and exact temperature. The event-noun rule supplies boiling.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The sound and motion of a pot told a cook when water had reached a boil long before a numbered instrument stood beside the hearth.

semantic_domains.physical: a liquid state with continuous rising bubbles
